item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hello there, I found the source of the image. It's a poster made by the russian government in 1986, so I think that it is still possible to use it. The image is the 20.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article was a pretty good idea but it was a mess. The grammar was highly inconsistent and it was riddled with contradictions and plot holes, as well as stylistic issues. I tried to make it read well without reference to the original poster which has been removed for non-compliance.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intriguing ideas, and I think it could be improved. I arrived here trough a list of "best SCPs" that someone posted elsewhere.

+ Criticisms
- I hope you don't get mad at me!

As others had previously noted, the writing is not well paced, and the background information could be better, or it falls in the category of *strange place with strange living beings* just for the sake of being strange. No explanation is given to why they attacked the D-class, or why they didn't breach from the station, before the Foundation knew about it.

The doctor in the audio logs are too detached from what is happening with the D-class personnel. And, to top it off, the last entry in the journal seems to be more what the guy is thinking then what he would write in a journal, which made me disconnect from my suspension of disbelief.

Also, the russian message in the image could be translated in the figure caption.

And why Greece?

+ Theory about SCP background
- But I think I am wrong!

In my mind, in some "other place" the idea of a Russian communist government created a society that tried to be totally equal. So people had no facial features, no one of them would seat in the train (so no one have privileges over the others). In each place, there can only be the exact number of people needed to work, so the number of people who hop on and off the train are the same.

The idea of the pigs are a little surreal, and to much Animal Farm-sque for me. If I follow with my idea, when the people started to become too much equal, they started to become pigs. The subway station becomes made of flesh to represent that society is being created from the flesh and bone from its workers. Then, in the next iteration, they became invisible to show that the individual person doesn't matter anymore…

Finally, when someone from outside appears, it must be "standardized". But, because they don't have eyes, they take some time to perceive the strangers.

I still don't know what vote to give…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP remember me the novel Metro 2033 by Dmitry Glukhovsky. Are these things the Dark Ones?

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This didn't do anything for me. Was the Russian blatantly wrong on purpose? Especially the second phrase.

And maybe I'm being dumb, but this didn't seem really all that… connected? All of it was just kind of random ~spooky~ description. None of it actually scared me.

I'm on the verge of a down vote, but I can't decide. I feel like I'm missing something that might make it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just so you know the title in scp database reads "Kind Men's". If you intended it to be "kind men", then "kind men" translated to russian is Добрые Люди (добрые - Kind, люди- people/men).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed the number of entities entering and exiting the trains remained balanced, if you include the D-classes in the count.

This lends it a creepy hint of "Hello, we've been expecting you."

I hope that was on purpose, and if so, I would have liked to see a little more done with it. As it is, "We've been expecting you" doesn't quite fit well with "…but we don't actually want you to do anything here, and will clobber you if you try."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aren't there any errors in documentation? The first log is Document 2727-1, the second is nameless, while the closing report is Document 2727-1 again.

There were also some errors in a sentence with two verbs, I dared fix them (sorry).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked reading this, Russian Propaganda Slendermen is just to weird not to upvote on concept alone.

Also, that part with the humanoid pigs reminded me a lot of Animal Farm, though that might be intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing about this that I might even possibly get is that the faceless ones were capitalist pigs or something? I don't know.
Please explain what the heck is going on behind the scenes here, I don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like a lot of weird for the sake of weird, which I don't have a problem with per se. But there were also some weird tone issues, which when combined with the fact that faceless humanoids feel kind of overdone (the fact that so many comments here referenced Slenderman is not a great sign) and I felt like I had to downvote. With a little tightening I could see myself going neutral but this just isn't my particular cup of tea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love this propaganda. Good job, Jabonicus, for getting this into my Top SCPs list.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have absolutely no clue what you are trying to say here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking it was a Slenderman train for a moment :D
SCP Foundation can handle this :D
1. Use the Tree to clone some D Class Staff, since they will die in a Week anyways.
2. Arm them with Nuke Collars and Surveillance equipment… the collars go off if they die.
3. Do some Exploring :D
4. If Steps 1-3 are not possible, put SCP-[Retracted] on a train and have SCP Staff take it off at a Reasonable station, and open it, so the Feline People can be let off, no longer your problem.
5. If possible, gather as much information, then perform step 4 if safe to send the poor kitties there.
6. Prepare for possible retaliation by SCP-2727

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The bit about the holes unnerved me a bit. And while holes do scare me, faceless russian propaganda monsters scare me even more. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this legitimately creepy. Why did I read this at 22:45 hours?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhh, it's not ambiguous enough to interest me and it's not explicit enough to scare me. I don't dislike it, however. No vote.

item.title: Re: slenderwalking my ass!
item.content:encoded:
There are actually more than a dozen different spoken "Chinese" languages, several of which are mutually incomprehensible. They all use the same written language, however, by fiat of the central government. Mandarin is one of the main ones, yes, but Cantonese, Shanghainese and Taiwanese are other big ones.

I do agree that it should be more specific than just "shouted something in Chinese", though.

item.title: slenderwalking my ass!
item.content:encoded:
You said the chinese guy shouted something in chinese…
Dont chinese people speak mandarin?
Im from South Africa yet I dont speak south african.
Sorry XD. Just a sucker for the details

+1 because i found slender man's origin!
Its not actually one guy who teleports, its a shitload of them taking a train!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Faceless people.
SLENDERMAN.

Sorry had to say it. Anyways, I liked it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of a CreepyPasta called "The Strangers" I read a while back. I like the idea here, but I honestly don't get what's going on at all. Is the subway car the scp? Does an entire subway tunnel appear containing these SCPs? What are the faceless things, and why do they become pigs? I'm not going to downvote for my own lack of understanding though.
I do feel the dialogue needs to be fixed up, however. I understand that the researchers have to be cold and follow procedures, but his tone just comes across as entirely too robotic.
I am digging it though, neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not exactly sure how they deal with translations over there, but if it is just translated with no other thoughts, this article is going to confuse a lot of people. This article is based mainly for people who don't speak Russian. So this article might be less popular over there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what's the title gonna be on the russian wiki?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A mistake. Will fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first you say his name is Dr. Wallace, but then you black-box it. Is that a mistake or is it another doctor?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the imagery here a lot, but in the end I feel like it's weird stuff just for the sake of weird stuff, not because there was some underlying reason for it. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's supposed to say "of the good men" not just "the good men."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FYI: "Добрых мужчин" is genitive case of "the good men". You may want to change it to "Добрые мужчины", which is infinitive.
In case anyone is curious, the first poster translates to "Strength in unity".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, Line Dancing Slendermen Spooky Corporate Railway Adventures makes its triumphant appearance! Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took away the last part because it does not go with the article anymore, it was from the first draft of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image was bothering me until the Russian showed up in the exploration log. I agree that some indication of the discrepancy would be nice (is there a station where everything's in Russian?) This is just this side of creepy enough for an upvote, though you need a serious editing pass. Also:

Instances of SCP-2727-2 typically resemble Homo sapiens, but lack any facial features. Instances of SCP-2727-2 lack navels or any other characteristics which would imply mammalian birth. Instances of SCP-2727-2 are typically docile.

Please condense this into one sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's certainly creepy, but the doctor's dialogue seems almost… mechanical (and repetitive, he jumps between 'relevant' and 'irrelevant' several times)

Fix the tone issues with the dialogue and I'll upvote.

Neutral vote until then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since you translated the Russian on the second poster, I think you should consider adding a translation of the first one in its caption. Also, while you definitely shouldn't explain it, I think you should consider at least pointing out the discrepancy of finding Russian in and around an SCP mainly located in Greece.

Additionally, the containment procedures and the first paragraph of the description have a lot of sentences that were very difficult for me to process. This caused a lot of hang-ups at the beginning and forced me to have to restart a few times, almost making me stop reading entirely.

Really enjoyed the callousness of the doctor and the horror of the transformation process. I like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-203583: Do you want me to just stand here with this things?

Is he supposed to say "this things?" instead of "these things?" here? Otherwise, all aboard the crazy train!

Note to self, don't get on trains. Nothing good will come from them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll add something in the description when I get home, on mobile right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just realized; in containment procedures you say instances of SCP-272727-2 are to be terminated on-site.

Doesn't that mean we have one or more of their bodies and thus would have dissected them to find out what of?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting and strange. Nice work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I think this is one of the best new SCP I've read recently. Very creepy, bery well written. I'd love to see more from this SCP (maybe in a tale or somefin) and more like this!

You've earned your upvote, good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's neat, but a few problems are making me downvote:

  • Are the subway cars the SCPs? Or is the presence of a subway system on an island the SCP? Or is it the stations? You don't say what's anomalous about the trains, so they don't really deserve a designation.
  • Like Bryx said, the dialogue bothers me. You'd think they'd try to comfort the D-Class a bit more so he doesn't do something stupid in fear, but instead they keep him entirely in the dark.
  • The humanoids. Hell, the researcher says they have vital signs, but you don't really explain if they're alive or not.

Fix these issues, and I'll reverse my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes I am, I did not too long ago, you just don't remember it was me.
Or it wasn't me?

Do we even know?

/me explodes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You aren't the first person to call me soap

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess the Spanish soap reference is just a wink.
You're welcome, this is nice, very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the repetitiveness is a recurring problem for me, I'm working on it. I'll fix the dialogue when I can think of a way to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A refreshingly scary skip. Very well done.

Just two nitpicks: Your sentence structure is kind of repetitive, and some parts of the doctor's dialogue feel a bit snarky at times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Spanish soap has returned with 2727! Anyway I posted this before without an actual story, so I fixed that along with a few other problems.
